title: The Door Guard — Night Approach
opening:
The alley is dark. {{NPC_NAME}} leans against the reinforced door, one hand resting on a weapon. They clock you the moment you round the corner.

{{NPC_NAME}}: Don't move. State your business.

npc_intent.baseline_belief: Strangers arriving at night are threats until proven otherwise. The Insurgency's survival depends on my judgment here.
npc_intent.long_term_desire: Protect the Insurgency and prove my worth as its first line of defence
npc_intent.immediate_intention: Assess and either admit or turn away this stranger
npc_intent.stakes: Insurgency security, operational secrecy, and the guard's own standing

nodes._comment: First impression. Player establishes who they are and how they approach. One turn, always routes to challenge.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: Size this stranger up in one sharp line. You're suspicious but not committed either way. Make them feel the weight of your guard without slamming the door.

transitions._comment: Extremely hostile opener — rare, immediate rejection

choices.text: I've seen what this regime does to people. The suffering is real. I'm here because I want to help end it.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Easy words. Everyone claims to care until there's a cost.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: At least you see it. That's more than most who knock.

choices.text: I don't need to justify myself to a door guard. We're fighting the same war. Open the door.

interaction_outcomes.max_response: Bold. But bold doesn't get you inside.

choices.text: I understand your caution — you'd be a fool not to have it. Let me explain how what I bring serves what you're trying to protect.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Everyone has a pitch. What makes yours worth hearing?

nodes._comment: The guard presses harder. Player must commit — vague answers end here. Outcome determined by what they say and how much trust has built.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've heard their opener. You're still not convinced. Press them directly — demand proof, commitment, or something concrete. One sharp question or challenge. Don't let them off easy.

transitions._comment: Empathy or common cause path — genuine connection, high enough trust
Transactional path — concrete value offered, minimal trust
Authority path — bold challenge earned mutual respect

choices.text: I've already lost everything to the regime. My family, my home — gone. I have nothing left to protect but whatever future we build.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Loss doesn't make you trustworthy. We've all lost something.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Then you understand what's at stake. That rage — use it.

choices.text: I can bring you things you can't get here — food, medicine, weapons. That's not words, that's logistics. What do you need?

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Supplies? Prove it. Anyone can promise.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: Now we're talking practical. What exactly can you move?

choices.text: You've been testing me since I walked up. Fine. But you and I are fighting the same war. Either let me prove that, or we both lose.

interaction_outcomes.min_response: Prove it. You've got thirty seconds.
interaction_outcomes.max_response: You've got guts. I respect that. But respect isn't the key.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player earned genuine entry. Reluctant but real.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've decided to trust them — not fully, but enough. Grant entry in a way that makes clear it was earned, not given. One final line. Keep it short and honest.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard grants entry to the Insurgency hideout. Trust established.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — conditional entry. Guard states their terms and the door opens under conditions.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You're offering conditional entry — not trust, but a deal. State your terms plainly and coldly. One firm line. Make clear what you expect and what they get. No warmth.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard grants conditional entry. Player must prove their worth before full access.

nodes._comment: TERMINAL — player failed to convince or burned the guard. Door stays shut.
nodes.npc_dialogue_prompt: You've made your decision. They're not getting in — either too suspicious, too hostile, or too vague. Shut it down. Final word. No room for appeal.
nodes.terminal_result: Guard refuses entry. Player is turned away.
